title: Modeling ecological drivers in marine viral communities using comparative metagenomics and network analyses
author: Bonnie Hurwitz
license_note: Confirmed via CrossRef DOI metadata: dx.doi.org/10.17504/protocols.io.efgbbjw
materials: Log into to iPlant/CyVerse (http://www.cyverse.org/, http://de.iplantcollaborative.org) Discovery Environment.
a Optional args: K-mer size: Default is

steps.title: Upload sequence and metadata files
steps.instruction: Upload FASTA-formatted sequence files and a tab-delimited file of metadata. Example data can be found in the Data Store at "/iplant/home/shared/imicrobe/fizkin/pov." To view in the Discovery Environment: Click on the "Data" button in the DE Go to the "Community Data -> imicrobe -> fizkin -> pov -> fasta" directory a A sample metadata file is also included ("meta.tab"). The headers of the metadata file should include the 'name' of the file and fields ending in '.d' for 'discrete' value (e.g., 'Male' or 'Female'), '.c' for 'continuuous' data (e.g., numbers in a range), or '.ll' for 'latitude/longitude' data. Field names should not include underscores with the exception of 'lat_long.ll.' Here is an example table: a

steps.title: Values between 16 and 31 are best. Mode minimum
steps.instruction: Values between 16 and 31 are best. Mode minimum: Default is

steps.title: Increase to require more stringent matching
steps.instruction: Increase to require more stringent matching. Max. num. sequences: Default is 300K. Use a lower value to reduce runtime. Use a higher value to get deeper coverage. Samples containing more than this parameter will be randomly sampled. Max. num. samples: Default is

steps.title: Keep in mind that Fizkin runs...
steps.instruction: Keep in mind that Fizkin runs a pair-wise analysis, so runtime is O(n^2). If your number of samples is greater than this argument, the samples will be randomly selected. Files list: The subset of files you wish to run, one file on each line

steps.title: Launch analysis
steps.instruction: Press "Launch analysis" and wait for notification of the completion of your job.

steps.title: Verify results
steps.instruction: The ultimate result should be a social network graph showing the grouping of samples similar to this: a

description: Common failures include something like this from R (GBME): Error in summary(fit1)$cov.unscaled[(2 * n):length(fit1)$coef, (2 * n):length(fit1)$coef] : subscript out of bounds Calls: gbme -> gbme.glmstart -> as.matrix Execution halted This is usually due to the metadata being too homogenous or entirely heterogenous. Remove any offending metadata and try again.
